headline: Broadcom gets major OpenAI boost in AI chip race
summary: Broadcom (AVGO) just got a new validation point in the artificial intelligence chip race: OpenAI’s first custom chip, Jalapeño, unveiled on June 24. The chip was designed with Broadcom and built for inference, the computing process that allows products such as ChatGPT to respond to user requests. ...

headline: Nvidia Stock Gains but AI Chip Rivals Are Circling
summary: Nvidia  was rising early Thursday.  The chip maker was benefiting from widespread semiconductor cheer, even as more options came onto the artificial-intelligence processor market.  Nvidia shares were up 0.9% at $200.76 in premarket trading with chip stocks generally gaining after  Micron Technology  blowout earnings.

headline: Why Goldman is cautious on outperforming Intel stock
summary: Investing.com -- Goldman Sachs initiated coverage of Intel with a Neutral rating and a 12-month price target of $150, acknowledging clear tailwinds in server CPUs and foundry optionality but arguing that the stock's recent run has already priced in much of the upside.

content.title: Why Goldman is cautious on outperforming Intel stock
content.summary: Investing.com -- Goldman Sachs initiated coverage of Intel with a Neutral rating and a 12-month price target of $150, acknowledging clear tailwinds in server CPUs and foundry optionality but arguing that the stock's recent run has already priced in much of the upside.

headline: Nvidia Stock Slips Below $200, and the Competition Fears Are Growing
summary: Nvidia  was falling early Thursday after rising throughout premarket trading as initial semiconductor cheer from Micron earnings quickly reversed.  Nvidia shares were down 2% at $2194.98 in early trading after  Micron Technology  blowout earnings.  As Barron’s has written, Nvidia looked to be establishing a trading range with a floor of roughly $200, after breaking through that level in April.

headline: Nvidia Stock Slips Below $200. Competition Fears Are Growing.
summary: Nvidia  slid on Thursday as the initial boost from  Micron’s  blowout earnings report faded, giving way to deeper anxieties about artificial-intelligence stocks.  Extending its longest losing streak since May, Nvidia stock fell for the fourth day straight, dropping 1.8% to $195.47.  As Barron’s has written, Nvidia looked to be establishing a trading range with a floor of roughly $200, after breaking through that level in April.

content.title: Nvidia Stock Slips Below $200. Competition Fears Are Growing.
content.summary: Nvidia  slid on Thursday as the initial boost from  Micron’s  blowout earnings report faded, giving way to deeper anxieties about artificial-intelligence stocks.  Extending its longest losing streak since May, Nvidia stock fell for the fourth day straight, dropping 1.8% to $195.47.  As Barron’s has written, Nvidia looked to be establishing a trading range with a floor of roughly $200, after breaking through that level in April.

headline: The Third Wave of AI: Why It’s Time for Investors to Pivot From ‘Infrastructure’ to ‘Efficiency’
